steps.title: Recognize the scale factor rule for areas
steps.content: The side length of square A is 246 times the side length of square B. For any two similar figures, when linear dimensions scale by a factor $k$, the areas scale by the square of that factor. Here, $k = 246$.

steps.title: Calculate the area scale factor and match the answer
steps.content:
The area of square A is $246^2$ times the area of square B.
Computing: $246^2 = 60,516$
That matches choice A — A is correct.
